mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.37% as of 2021-11-15 indicates ultra-loose monetary stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month employment growth measured by PAYEMS above 200k signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 143,894k as of 2021-11-15 reflects post-pandemic recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with strong labor demand
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending measured by RSAFS combined with accelerating housing starts (HOUST) signals demand-side momentum that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both series show month-over-month growth.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 455,644M and HOUST at 1,172k as of 2021-11-15
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or joint effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 103.0 signals supply-side weakness that constrains earnings growth and increases recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 103.0 or declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.55 as of 2021-11-15 near recent highs
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 5-day returns in fewer than 50% of cases, or Rank IC remains above -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 5.0% combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) above 4.5% signals stagflation regime where the Fed faces policy dilemma, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days due to margin compression and policy uncertainty.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 5.0% AND UNRATE rises above 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 238.89 and UNRATE at 4.7% as of 2021-11-15
failure_condition: Stagflation signal produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured at quarterly frequency above 2.5% annualized signals sustained economic expansion that supports equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days in expansion regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,505.1B (2017 dollars) as of 2021-10-15 reflects strong recovery
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns during expansion periods are not significantly higher than contraction periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3pp over 3-month period
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% as of 2021-11-15 remains near cycle lows
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of cases, or Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals tight supply-demand balance that supports pricing power and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both conditions hold simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth positive AND UNRATE declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.55 and UNRATE at 4.7% as of 2021-11-15
regime_hint: tight labor market with supply-side strength
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 53% of triggered instances, or the joint effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.
